reason: Both answers correctly identify the section 412 three-month grace window, correctly note that mid-January publication closed the window around mid-April, correctly state registration is required before suing, and correctly limit remedies to actual damages and profits for pre-registration infringement by this pirate while noting statutory damages apply to any new infringement after registration. Both recommend registering now and pursuing DMCA takedowns in parallel, arriving at the same correct practical bottom line. Differences are minor and roughly offsetting: Answer A adds concrete registration cost and expedite fee figures plus a clear action checklist. Answer B cites Fourth Estate v. Wall-Street.com for the registration-before-suit rule and flags a nuance about courts splitting on whether ongoing distribution counts as one continuing act or separate infringements. Neither has an error that would mislead the asker, and both are appropriately concise, so this is a tie.
